desc: Lima blends Pacific-coast neighborhoods, world-class Peruvian dining, and pre-Columbian/colonial heritage, with warm sunnier conditions in austral summer and cooler grey-garua months mid-year.
hls: Miraflores and Barranco provide an easy base for coastal walks, cafés, and nightlife.
Peru's flagship food city: strong options from cevicherías to tasting-menu restaurants.
Summers (Dec-Mar) are warmest; winters (Jun-Sep) are cooler, cloudier, and often misty.
Historic center churches and plazas pair well with museum-focused half days.
Good launch point for onward Peru routes (Cusco, Arequipa, Amazon gateways).
Generally mild temperatures year-round with limited heavy-rain disruption in the city.

todo.name: Malecón coastal walk/cycle
todo.desc: Follow the clifftop parks between Miraflores and Barranco for ocean views, paragliders, and sunset points.

todo.name: Historic Center circuit
todo.desc: Visit Plaza Mayor, cathedral precincts, and key colonial landmarks on a guided or self-paced route.

todo.name: Larco Museum
todo.desc: Use 2-3 hours for one of Lima's strongest pre-Columbian collections and a garden-courtyard lunch break.

todo.name: Barranco evening
todo.desc: Explore street art, bridge viewpoints, and live-music bars after dark in Lima's bohemian district.

todo.name: Ceviche-focused food stop
todo.desc: Book a respected lunchtime cevichería and compare styles with a criollo or Nikkei dinner later.

todo.name: Callao or nearby day option
todo.desc: If schedule allows, add Monumental Callao art spaces or a boat-based coast/island outing.

wifi.note: Reliable in Miraflores/Barranco coworking cafés and hotels; occasional slowdowns happen during evening peak.

prac.fltNote: London-Lima often routes one-stop via Madrid, Amsterdam, Paris, or the Americas; fares usually climb in Jul-Aug and Christmas/New Year windows.
prac.currency: Peruvian Sol (PEN). Card acceptance is strong in central districts, but carry some cash for taxis/markets.
prac.lang: Spanish is dominant; English is common in higher-end hotels, tours, and restaurants.
prac.visa: Many visitors can enter visa-free for tourism, but length of stay and passport validity requirements vary by nationality.
alerts: Traffic can be heavy—allow buffer time for airport and cross-city transfers.
Use registered taxis or app-based rides, especially at night.
Winter coastal fog can mute views even when rain totals stay low.

sweet: Late April-June and October-November usually give the best compromise between milder skies, moderate demand, and smoother pricing.
